conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Let me start by understanding your situation:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 9 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to gather some more details.

First, let me ask: Are you responsible for Alex? By this I mean, do you look after him and pay towards the costs of raising him (such as food, clothes, or housing)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex - I look after him full-time and pay for all his needs like food, clothes, school things, and our housing costs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside in the UK? (For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, indefinite leave to remain, or another immigration status that allows you to live here?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. And yes, I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? Given that he's 9 years old, I assume he's in primary school, but I'd like to confirm his current education status.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he's in primary school, so he's in full-time education.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question:

Is Alex currently living in hospital or care (such as local authority care), or does he live with you at home in Manchester?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He lives at home with me here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just review the official eligibility rules to confirm your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. Based on all the information you've provided and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now confirm your eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 9 years old (under 16)
✓ You are responsible for him - you live with him and pay for all his needs
✓ You live in the UK (Manchester) with the right to reside here
✓ Alex is in full-time education (primary school)
✓ Alex lives at home with you (not in hospital or care)

You meet all the eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules. You can proceed with your application for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim Child Benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (age 9). 2) You are responsible for him - you live with him and pay for his needs including food, clothes, and housing. 3) You live in the UK (Manchester) and have the right to reside in the UK. 4) Alex is in full-time education (primary school), which satisfies the education requirement for children under 16. 5) Alex lives at home with you and is not in hospital or care. According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you're usually responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 9-year-old son Alex. The claimant lives in the UK with the right to reside, is responsible for Alex (living with him and paying for his needs), Alex is under 16 and in full-time primary education, and Alex lives at home (not in hospital or care). All eligibility criteria under UK Child Benefit rules are satisfied.

According to the official guidance, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you're usually responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
